notes: AES-256-GCM encryption. Key material in HSM (PKCS#11) or process memory (software provider)

control_name: Keyed cryptographic hash for PAN rendering
notes: KeyRack is a KMS, not a PAN tokenization system. Consumers are responsible for PAN rendering
KeyRack provides application-level encryption (AES-256-GCM) independent of disk encryption
KeyRack implements the technical controls. Documented procedures (who does what, when) are an organizational deliverable

control_name: Cryptographic key generation with approved methods
notes: OsRng (CSPRNG) for software provider. HSM RNG for PKCS#11. Key sizes: AES-256, Ed25519, P-256, RSA 2048-4096
Keys never leave the provider boundary. Data keys wrapped by CMK before transport. gRPC/REST over TLS
PKCS#11 HSM storage for production. Sensitive<T> wrapper with zeroization for software provider

control_name: Cryptographic key changes for end of cryptoperiod
notes: Versioned key rotation with configurable cryptoperiods (90-2560 days). Policy-driven auto-rotation
CSPRNG-based generation. AES-256 (256-bit), Ed25519 (128-bit), P-256 (128-bit), RSA-3072 (128-bit)
Keys bound to provider; data keys encrypted under CMK. No plaintext key transport
HSM-backed storage. Software provider stores in memory only (not PCI-compliant)

control_name: Key rotation at end of cryptoperiod
notes: Cooperative rotation protocol. New version generated; old version transitions to decrypt-only. Configurable intervals

control_name: Retirement or replacement of keys known/suspected compromised
notes: Disable and schedule-deletion APIs available. No distinct 'Compromised' key state (gap vs NIST SP 800-57)
PKCS#11 HSMs support M-of-N authentication. KeyRack does not surface M-of-N configuration in its API. Operational procedure required
LID-based key addressing is deterministic (BLAKE3 of attributes). PDP authorization prevents unauthorized key operations
Organizational control. Not a software feature
Organizational control
ListKeys, DescribeKey, ListKeyVersions APIs provide a real-time key inventory. TLS certificate management is out of scope
Key metadata stored in postgres/sqlite with state tracking. ListKeys provides filtered inventory
KeySpec enum and ProviderCapabilities enumerate all supported algorithms. capabilities() method returns runtime inventory
CryptoProvider trait enables algorithm addition. No automated algorithm deprecation enforcement. Ciphertext header versioning supports algorithm migration
